title: MEDDPICC Deal Review
description: Complete MEDDPICC deal review with qualification scoring, stakeholder analysis, sales strategy, and close plan. Used by the meddpicc Claude Code plugin for guided deal qualification, JSON data capture, and XLS rendering.

metadata.description: Deal identification, status, financials, client interactions, Salesforce integration, and completion tracking

dealId.description: Unique identifier for this deal review

dealStatus.description: Current deal stage from pick list

winProbability.description: Win probability as decimal 0-1. Common values from pick list: 0.05, 0.10, 0.25, 0.35, 0.50, 0.60, 0.75

salesforce.description: Optional Salesforce integration fields — present when SFDC data has been imported

lastSyncDate.description: Last time data was synced from Salesforce

completionStatus.description: Tracks which sections are complete for resume support. The AI reads this to determine where to pick up.

qualification.description: The 8 MEDDPICC qualification elements with embedded questions, scoring rubrics, and responses

definition.const: Quantified business outcomes the client expects — cost reduction, risk reduction, revenue impact, productivity gains, time-to-value

default: What goals is the client trying to accomplish?
What KPIs are they using to measure the goals?

default.0: Unknown — No metrics identified or discussed
default.1: Identified — Qualitative goals mentioned but no specific KPIs or baselines
default.2: Validated — KPIs documented and confirmed with at least one stakeholder
default.3: Quantified — Metrics tied to business value with baseline and target data
default.4: Committed — Client has committed to metrics in writing or proposal

definition.const: The individual with final budget authority and political power to fund the purchase

default: How are we engaged with them to address their priorities?

default.0: Unknown — Don't know who controls budget
default.1: Identified — Role identified but not the specific person; access only through intermediaries
default.2: Engaged — EB identified by name; no direct access yet but plan exists
default.3: Aligned — Direct access achieved; EB priorities and personal win documented
default.4: Sponsoring — EB is actively sponsoring the deal through procurement

definition.const: The requirements the customer will use to evaluate options — technical, business, operational, and commercial

default: What criteria is the client using to evaluate the proposition?
What is the client's budget or business case?

default.0: Unknown — Don't know how the customer will evaluate solutions
default.1: Identified — General requirements known but not documented or ranked
default.2: Validated — Criteria partially documented with some influence actions taken
default.3: Differentiated — Written criteria with priority ranking; competitive position mapped per criterion
default.4: Locked — Criteria influenced toward our strengths; key stakeholders aligned

definition.const: The step-by-step path from evaluation to signed agreement

default: What is the client's technical approval process for the solution you are proposing?
What is the client's business approval process and typical timeline for a transaction of this nature?

default.0: Unknown — Unknown how the customer will make and approve the decision
default.1: Identified — High-level understanding only; no dates or named owners
default.2: Mapped — Major steps identified but dates or owners missing
default.3: Validated — Step-by-step process documented with named owners and dates
default.4: Controlled — All gates mapped; no surprises expected; legal/security/procurement flow confirmed

definition.const: The procurement mechanics — quotes, approvals, vendor onboarding, security review, legal redlines, signature process

default: What are the legal steps required to conclude on the transaction?
What administrative steps need to be completed to allow an order to flow?

default.1: Identified — Aware that procurement exists but steps unknown
default.2: Mapped — Some procurement steps known; security requirements identified
default.3: In Progress — Procurement steps documented with owners; security artifacts submitted; legal in review
default.4: Complete — All procurement, legal, and security steps done; signature workflow mapped; PO format confirmed

definition.const: The compelling business pain that creates urgency to act

default: What is the problem the client is solving for and how does it impact them?
What happens if the client can't solve the problem?

default.0: Unknown — No pain identified or discussed; solution looking for a problem
default.1: Identified — Pain implied but not explicitly confirmed by customer
default.2: Acknowledged — Pain acknowledged by customer but not fully quantified
default.3: Quantified — Pain quantified with business impact; consequence of inaction documented
default.4: Urgent — Multiple stakeholders confirm pain; triggering event identified; clear deadline driver

definition.const: A powerful internal advocate who sells for you when you're not in the room

default: Who will benefit most from this transaction?
Can they influence the buying process?

default.1: Identified — Friendly contact without organizational power; passive supporter
default.2: Tested — Friendly contact with some influence; willing to help but hasn't taken significant action
default.3: Validated — Named individual with influence and credibility; personal win documented
default.4: Mobilizing — Champion is actively coaching you, sharing intel, and taking concrete actions weekly

definition.const: Any alternative the buyer might choose — another vendor, internal build, or do nothing

default: Who are we competing with and what is their strategy?
What are their strengths and weaknesses?

default.1: Identified — Assumptions about competition; no differentiation strategy
default.2: Assessed — Competitors identified but customer perception unclear; general differentiation known
default.3: Differentiated — Competitors named and qualified; customer perception documented; differentiation strategy clear
default.4: Neutralized — Competitive proof delivered; do-nothing risk assessed; win theme validated with customer

threeWhys.description: F5-specific: Why Anything, Why Us, Why Now — for both F5 and the partner

whyAnything.description: Why does the client need to change from the status quo?

whyF5.description: Why is F5 the right choice over alternatives?

whyNow.description: Why must this happen on the current timeline?

whyAnything.description: Why does the partner need to engage?

whyThisPartner.description: Why is this the right partner?

whyNow.description: Why must the partner engage now?

stakeholders.description: Stakeholder analysis grid — all individuals involved in the buying decision

mustSayYes.description: Must this person say yes for the deal to proceed?

canSayNo.description: Can this person veto or block the deal?

whatTheyNeedToBelieve.description: What this person needs to believe to say yes to the deal

f5Owner.description: F5 team member responsible for this relationship

differentiatedValueProposition.description: What is our differentiated value proposition?

closePlan.description: Customer-agreed milestones and critical actions to close

criticalActions.description: Critical actions and next steps to support close plan

team.description: F5 and partner team members assigned to the deal

scoring.description: Computed scoring summary — derived from qualification.*.score values. Updated by the AI after each section.

previousElementScores.description: Snapshot of element scores from the previous review — used by deal-review to compute score deltas

overallScore.description: Weighted average across 8 elements as percentage: (sum of scores / 32) * 100

overallRating.description: Traffic light based on raw score: Red (0-13/32, ~0-41%), Yellow (14-25/32, ~44-78%), Green (26-32/32, ~81-100%). Use raw score brackets as the definitive boundary.

definition.description: What this element means in the MEDDPICC framework

questions.description: Guiding questions the AI asks the rep

responses.description: Rep's answers, ordered to match questions array

scoreDefinition.description: What each score level means for this specific element
